title: SpaceX lists on Nasdaq in the largest public offering in history
summary: The American space company raised about 12 trillion yen, the highest figure in an IPO.

title: 2026 World Cup begins with opening matches and expectations for Japan
summary: The tournament kicked off in Mexico with local victories, criticism of streaming costs, and extensive media coverage in Japan.

title: Trump cancels attack on Iran and says a deal is near
summary: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz continue as the United States and Iran negotiate a possible agreement.

title: Japan successfully launches H3 rocket after overcoming previous failures
summary: The new-generation rocket placed six satellites into orbit, marking a milestone for Japan's space program.

title: Iconic British painter David Hockney dies at 88
summary: The artist, famous for his colorful landscapes and portraits, leaves an important legacy in contemporary art.

title: Japanese actress Tamao Nakamura dies at 86
summary: Known for her versatility in film and television, her death was reported by Japanese media.

title: Shohei Ohtani leaves game due to left knee inflammation
summary: The Dodgers player left the game early as a precautionary measure, according to the team.

title: Japanese Lower House committee approves bill to reform criminal review system
summary: The bill seeks to improve transparency in reopening cases, although it generates controversy.

title: Japan revises its preparedness plan for a major earthquake in Tokyo
summary: The government updated the guidelines, but challenges remain in implementation and public awareness.

title: Yohei Kono, former Speaker of the Japanese House of Representatives, dies
summary: Kono, also chairman of the International Trade Association, died, affecting the diplomatic agenda.

title: Thai princess dies after three years in unconscious state
summary: The Thai royal princess died after being in a coma for more than three years.

title: U.S. Supreme Court halts execution by nitrogen inhalation
summary: The court considered that the method could be cruel, blocking the execution at the last minute.

title: South Korean prosecution accuses North Korea of sending drones to justify martial law
summary: The investigation indicates that the drones were prepared to create a pretext of emergency.

title: China detains American researcher in Myanmar on espionage charges
summary: The detention of the academic has generated diplomatic tensions between China and the United States.

title: Shiga nuclear plant raises maximum tsunami estimate to 12 meters
summary: The new projection exceeds the height of the ground, raising questions about the safety of the facility.

title: Japan issues first national guidelines on installation of women's restrooms
summary: The recommendations aim to reduce long lines at women's restrooms in public spaces.

title: PFAS detected in well at logistics center in Chiba at levels 1,200 times above limit
summary: The discovery of these persistent chemicals in groundwater could affect health.

title: Life insurance data leak scandal spreads to 14 companies in Japan
summary: A former employee allegedly stole information of thousands of clients, with the case expanding to multiple insurers.

title: Mos Burger and Marugame warn about fraudulent ads using their brands
summary: The fast-food chains warn consumers about fake promotions online.

title: Japanese lieutenant general explains how drones have changed warfare
summary: The Self-Defense Forces officer analyzes the evolution of the battlefield with drone technology.
